items.title: That's life: Derryn Hinch's most memorable media moments

children.content: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

articles.title: Hinch revealed a scenario that seemed too crazy to be true — but he was right

children.content: Derryn Hinch was not a hero to every survivor of sexual abuse, but in the decades when they lacked allies, he was their fiercest public advocate.

items.title: This Place: Redbank Ngarindjerri

children.content: A grandfather and daughter in South Australia's Riverland are on a journey to revive culture, connection to country and Ngarrindjeri language for the next generation.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.
